figure.title: Error Detected at Switch UALink TL
figure.purpose: Shows the system state when an error is detected at the UALink TL of a Switch port.

technical_summary: An error is detected within the UALink TL of the Switch (on the port facing ACC A).
The UALink TL and the corresponding UPLI Originator in the Switch are highlighted with dashed boxes.
A loopback or isolation symbol (purple arrow) is shown at the Switch's UPLI Originator, indicating it has entered isolation or drop mode for that port.

components.name: UALink TL (Switch)
components.description: The layer where the error is detected on the switch port.

components.name: UPLI Originator (Switch)
components.description: Enters isolation/drop mode in response to the TL error.

critical_facts: Switch ports can isolate themselves independently when a UALink TL error is detected, dropping traffic at the UPLI Originator level.
